The method `createWildCardedStringCondition` uses LIKE for pattern matching where the filter may contain wildcards at any position (not just prefix search), which can lead to poor performance on large datasets. Consider using a full-text search index (e.g., MySQL FULLTEXT) or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch for better performance.

The CONTAINS match type uses LIKE with wildcards for substring searching, which cannot use standard indexes and results in full table scans. Consider using a full-text search index (e.g., MySQL FULLTEXT, PostgreSQL tsvector) for better performance on large datasets.

The ENDS_WITH match type uses LIKE with a leading wildcard (e.g., '%pattern'), which prevents index usage and causes full table scans. Consider using a full-text search solution or a reverse index if suffix matching is frequently needed.

Using regular expressions for pattern matching bypasses database indexes and results in slow full table scans. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine or database-native full-text search capabilities instead.

The CHARS_ANYWHERE pattern constructs a LIKE expression with wildcards between each character (e.g., '%a%b%c%'), which cannot use indexes and will be extremely slow on large tables. Consider using a full-text search solution or trigram indexes for fuzzy matching.

Using LIKE with a pattern derived from user input containing wildcards (*) converted to SQL wildcards (%) performs inefficient full-text search that cannot use indexes effectively. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine or database-native full-text search capabilities instead of LIKE with wildcards.

Using NOT LIKE with a pattern derived from user input containing wildcards (*) converted to SQL wildcards (%) performs inefficient full-text search that cannot use indexes. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine or database-native full-text search capabilities instead of NOT LIKE with wildcards.

Using regular expressions for pattern matching (likeRegex) performs inefficient full-text search that cannot leverage database indexes. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine or database-native full-text search capabilities instead of regex matching.
